backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the AD TRIBE backend, defining data types (AdvertisingProfessional, EquipmentVendor, ShootLocation, ProductionHouse) and state maps, with CRUD operations for profiles, recommendations, connections, and search, protected by role-based access control.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping a single feature (updating LandingPage.tsx hero section with the full AD TRIBE intro paragraph) to its affected file, components, and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-to-two sentence summaries for each frontend source file, serving as a reference index for the project's frontend components, pages, hooks, and utilities.
frontend/index.css: Root CSS file importing Tailwind directives and defining CSS custom properties for the design system's color tokens in both light and dark modes.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the AD TRIBE app, loading Bebas Neue and Inter fonts from Google Fonts and mounting the React app via the root div.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that configures TanStack Router with routes for landing, profile, dashboard, and connections pages, and conditionally renders the ProfileSetup modal for authenticated users without a profile.
frontend/src/components/calendar/Availab: Interactive monthly calendar component that cycles date cells through available/booked/unavailable/unset states with color coding, month navigation, and an optional read-only mode.
Simple presentational component rendering color-coded legend items for the three availability statuses (available, booked, unavailable).
frontend/src/components/layout/AppLayout: Main application layout component that renders a sticky header with navigation links (Dashboard, Profile, Connections) and a login/logout button, wrapping page content in a centered container.
frontend/src/components/profiles/Locatio: Form component for creating/editing a shoot location profile, including fields for name, description, capacity, daily rate, and an availability calendar.
frontend/src/components/profiles/Product: Form component for creating a production house profile with company name and info fields, submitting data via the useSetProductionHouse mutation.
frontend/src/components/profiles/Profess: Form component for advertising professionals to set their profile with name, specialties (tag-based input), portfolio links, and an availability calendar.
frontend/src/components/profiles/VendorP: Form component for equipment vendors to manage their profile, including business name, a tag-based equipment inventory list, and an availability calendar.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: Modal dialog component for first-time users to select an account type and enter their name, blocking dismissal until the profile is saved via the backend mutation.
frontend/src/components/search/ProviderC: Card component that displays a provider's details (professional, vendor, or location) including availability, type-specific attributes, and a button to send a connection request.
frontend/src/components/search/SearchBar: Simple controlled search input component with a search icon, used to filter providers by name or keyword.
frontend/src/components/search/SearchFil: Renders a dropdown filter for selecting provider type (all, professional, vendor, or location) using a Select component.
frontend/src/components/search/SearchRes: Fetches and displays filtered search results for professionals, vendors, and locations in a responsive grid, with loading skeletons and an empty state.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider, exposing login/logout functions, identity state, and login status via a React context hook.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for CRUD operations on user profiles, advertising professionals, equipment vendors, shoot locations, and related entities via the ICP backend actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that configures Tailwind base styles and defines light/dark mode CSS custom properties for the AD TRIBE warm amber/charcoal design theme.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/ConnectionsPage.tsx: Displays a tabbed connections management page showing sent or received connection requests depending on whether the user is a production house or a service provider.
frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx: Production-house-only dashboard page that combines a search bar, provider type filter, and search results to find and connect with professionals, vendors, and locations.
frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx: Marketing landing page for AD Tribe that displays hero section, feature highlights, and stats, and redirects authenticated users to their dashboard or profile based on their account type.
frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx: Profile management page that renders the appropriate profile form component (Professional, Vendor, Location, or ProductionHouse) based on the authenticated user's profile type.
frontend/src/utils/dateHelpers.ts: Utility functions for converting between JavaScript Date objects and backend Time (nanosecond BigInt) values, plus helpers for date comparison, formatting, and range generation.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes, with sessionStorage persistence helpers for maintaining parameter values across navigation.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining the AD Tribe brand color palette (amber, charcoal, gold), custom fonts (Inter, Bebas Neue), shadows, border radii, and animation keyframes.
project_state.json: Project state metadata file tracking version, architectural notes, known issues, and implemented features for the AD Tribe professional networking platform.
